div.p: Key invariants (between init and finalize): buf_len ∈ [0, 63] · total_bits = Σ(len × 8) · H[0..7] valid hash state A full 64-byte block is always flushed immediately in sha256_update() — never left in buf[]
sha256_update() reads + writes: H[8] (via compress) · buf[] · buf_len · total_bits
sha256_finalize() reads + writes all fields: H[8] → final state → digest[32] buf[] → padding target buf_len → marker insert point total_bits → FIPS length field at buf[56..63]
sha256_init() writes all fields: H[i] ← SHA256_INITIAL_HASH[i] (i=0..7) buf_len ← 0 · total_bits ← 0 buf[] → memset 0 (defensive)
H[0]·H[1]·H[2]·H[3]·H[4]·H[5]·H[6]·H[7] 4 B each · 32 bytes total · bytes 0x00 – 0x1F Running hash state · read+write by sha256_compress()
One full 512-bit message block buffer · bytes 0x20 – 0x5F Accumulates partial input between update() calls buf[0..buf_len-1] valid · buf[buf_len..63] unspecified
bytes 0x60 – 0x67 (8 bytes on 64-bit) · Range: [0, 63] Never equals 64 — full block flushed immediately in update()
bytes 0x68 – 0x6F (8 bytes) · Σ(len × 8) across all update() calls Written to FIPS 180-4 length field in finalize() · Range: [0, 2⁶⁴−1]
